top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(2). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 175 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(1087x), Chintan-JOOLA(293x), Tmf-JOOLA(285x), Ironvarr-JOOLA(226x), Coach_Block-JOOLA(219x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 65 players with 5+ clips
top_strategic.evidence: 65 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(661), Chintan(186), Tmf(133), Coach_Block(115), Ironvarr(107)
top_strategic.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

top_strategic.title: Sell the Idea Lab itself — every facility gets their own AI research team
top_strategic.evidence: The Idea Lab is currently an internal tool for us. But imagine every Courtana facility owner getting their own instance: '7 AI researchers analyzed YOUR 200 clips overnight. Top finding: your Tuesday evening players are 2x more likely to use premium paddles.' The facility just clicks approve/dismiss. They're not buying data — they're buying decisions.

top_strategic.title: Give JOOLA a free real-time brand dashboard — in exchange for verified product catalog data
top_strategic.evidence: JOOLA is 43% of our detections. They'd kill for real-time visibility into where their paddles appear. Trade access for their product database — we get ground truth brand data, they get analytics.

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.12 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.12, speed_up=4.27, reset=3.88, volley=3.51, dink=2.92, drive=2.87, serve=2.18, unknown=1.14, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.99 (precision=0.99, recall=0.98)
top_data.evidence: Across 515 verified clips: 2045 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.986.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 365/515 verified clips — add disambiguation
top_data.evidence: JOOLA dominates 71% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
top_data.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

top_data.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 4283 clips with signatures avg 2.9 viral vs 3.9
top_data.evidence: 4283 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 2.9 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
top_data.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

all_findings.title: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
all_findings.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (554/6973 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), Onix (4/45 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=6419, Nike=685, PICKLEBALL=235, Reolink=132, HEAD=123.
all_findings.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.39 vs 4.37) + watchability (6.78 vs 2.43)
all_findings.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.39, watchability=6.78. Bottom 100: quality=4.37, watchability=2.43. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Worst predicted badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.961), hustler (F1=0.93)
all_findings.evidence: Lowest F1 badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.961), hustler (F1=0.93). These need prompt improvement.
all_findings.action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence: Avg rally: 15.6 shots. Long rallies (15+): 2193 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 66 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Cross-corpus competitive intelligence — analyze competitor public content
all_findings.evidence: Scrape MLP/PPA YouTube highlights, run the same Gemini analysis, compare. 'Here is what the pros use vs what your recreational players use.' That cross-corpus comparison is something nobody else can do because nobody else has the pipeline.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: competitive_intel play

all_findings.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 4185 clips (97.0%) — sell to brands
all_findings.evidence: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 4185 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
all_findings.action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

all_findings.title: Top content creators: PickleBill leads with 661 clips
all_findings.evidence:
PickleBill: 661 clips, viral=3.2, Steady Eddie
Chintan: 186 clips, viral=3.4, Dink Specialist
Tmf: 133 clips, viral=3.4, Steady Eddie
Coach_Block: 115 clips, viral=2.8, Steady Eddie
Ironvarr: 107 clips, viral=3.4, Kitchen Master
all_findings.action: Create player spotlight feature for top contributors

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'athletic' is 220.0x more likely in top clips (22.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 22.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Player equipment tagging — let players tag their own gear for rewards
all_findings.evidence: Build a 'What's in my bag?' feature. Players tag their paddle, shoes, etc. after each session. Reward with badges/stats. This crowd-sources brand detection AND creates engagement loop.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: crowdsource play

all_findings.title: Reverse data marketplace — brands submit product data TO us
all_findings.evidence: Instead of detecting brands, invite them to our platform. They upload their product catalog, we match it against detections. They pay for the matching service. Flips the model entirely.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: partnership_flip play
